methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised over $2B and currently trades at a $10B+ valuation. The peer universe in this space remains narrow: the broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises five identifiable private peers—Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, Aurora Innovation, and ideaForge—with no direct public comparables available for triangulation.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable here. The analysis identified only one same-stage peer, zero adjacent-stage peers, and two similar-tier peers, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable median derivation. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise, given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity across the broader category.

Without sufficient statistical coverage via peer multiples, the valuation cannot be supported through traditional comparable-company analysis at this time. Additional data inputs—such as discounted cash flow assumptions, revenue benchmarks from the identified private peers, or stage-appropriate venture return expectations—would be required to establish a credible valuation range. The key uncertainty is the scarcity of seasoned public or late-stage private comparables in autonomous systems, which limits the ability to calibrate Joby's $10B+ valuation against market precedent. A full analysis would benefit from expanding the dataset or employing alternative methods such as DCF or precedent M&A transactions.
